rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN FUND MANAGEMENT ONE LP, A CALIFORNIA VS. ANDERS FUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery From Defendant Paramount Estate Llc And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for May 15, 2026, line 1. Plaintiff American Bay Area Fund Management LLC's motion to compel Paramount Estate LLC's further responses to requests for production of documents, set two; to compel production; and for sanctions, is granted in part. The court finds good cause to compel Paramount to make full production. Paramount has not produced documents showing the deposit of plaintiff's $1 million investment. (Huet-Cox Decl., para. 5.) Paramount has not produced documents concerning a First Republic account where checks were paid on the project at issue. (Id., para. 6.) Paramount has not produced documents from Preferred Bank, which are responsive to earlier sets of discovery requests. (Id., para. 10.) Paramount is ordered to make full production, including of the missing documents discussed in this order, and to meet and confer in person or telephonically with plaintiff's counsel concerning the scope of its search and its reasonable diligence in satisfying its search and production obligations. The court concludes that Paramount's objections to RFP Nos. 102 and 103 lack merit. A request for bank records from a particular account is not especially burdensome. Plaintiff shows relevance. Paramount shall respond with a statement of compliance in full, but may condition production on the entry of a protective order. Paramount must amend within two weeks of this order. If Paramount seeks a protective order for any of the production ordered herein, it must meet and confer within one week about the terms of a protective order. Full production shall be made within 30 days. Paramount's discovery position on this motion lacks substantial justification, but the court reduces the requested sanction to an amount it deems reasonable on this motion. Paramount is ordered to pay $3000 to plaintiff within 30 days of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JASMINE GROTEGEER VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for May 15, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANTS SHARON ANDERSON, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JONATHAN NEWSOME'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants the City and County of San Francisco, Jonathan H. Newsome, and Sharon Anderson move for summary judgment. Their moving papers carry their burden as the moving party on summary judgment of presenting evidence to show that there is no triable issue of material fact and they are entitled to judgment as a matter of law. Plaintiff Jasmine Grotegeer has not opposed the motion and has not shown a triable issue of fact. The court therefore GRANTS the motion. Defendants shall submit a proposed judgment of dismissal. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: TERI KOSCINSKI ET AL VS. PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT As To Complaint Against Plaintiffs J. Koscinski, C.B., D. Bracelin, K.B., And/Or J. Bracelin Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for May 15, 2026, line 4. DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT As To Complaint Against Plaintiffs J. Koscinski, C.B., D. Bracelin, K.B., and/or J. Bracelin Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication. Defendant Pacific Bell Telephone Company moves for summary judgment on all claims brought against it by plaintiffs Jade Koscinski, Dallas Bracelin, Jennifer Bracelin, C.B., and K.B. The court uses plaintiffs' first names because they have common last names with each other and other parties in this case; the court intends no disrespect. The motion is unopposed and Pacific Bell carries its burden as the moving party of presenting evidence to show that there is no triable issue of material fact and it is entitled to judgment as a matter of law. Jade, Dallas, Jennifer, C.B., and K.B. have not opposed the motion and present no evidence showing a triable issue of fact. The motion is therefore granted, and Pacific Bell may present a judgment of dismissal as to the following claims: Jade, Dallas, Jennifer, C.B., and K.B.'s claims for negligence (first cause of action); negligent hiring, training, and supervision (second cause of action); and negligent infliction of emotional distress (fifth cause of action). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JENNY LEE VS. ATLANTIS TOWING INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for May 15, 2026, line 6. DEFENDANTS ATLANTIS TOWING INC, MOHAMMED ABUZAHRIYEH, MARVIN LARIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Amended). Defendants' motion for summary judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The record discloses that defendants inadvertently failed to attach several pieces of evidence cited in their separate statement of undisputed material facts. Defendants filed a notice of errata and an additional declaration to attempt to cure the defect. Plaintiff correctly notes that it is improper for defendants to introduce new reply evidence. (See CCP 437c(b)(4) ["The reply shall not include any new evidentiary matter, additional material facts, or separate statement submitted with the reply and not presented in the moving papers or opposing papers."].) The preference is to hear motions for summary judgment on the merits where there exists a curable procedural default. (Walton v. Victor Valley Community College District (2026) 119 Cal.App.5th 1164.) Since the trial date in this case was recently continued to November 2, 2026, the court denies the motion without prejudice to allow defendants an opportunity to cure the technical defects in their motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCUS DYLAN ROSARIO VS. SUBHI ABDELHALIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Admitted, Compel, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for May 15, 2026, line 11. PLAINTIFF FRANCISCUS ROSARIO'S Motion To Deem Admitted, Compel, And For Sanctions. The motion is denied. The court has granted defendants' anti-SLAPP motion, and discovery in this action has been stayed since defendants filed their notice of the anti-SLAPP motion. See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 425.16, subd. (g). In addition, the motion was served with insufficient notice. The Code required 16 court days plus two additional court days for electronic service. See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b); id., sec. 1010.6, subd. (a)(3)(B). The court awards reasonable sanctions of $200 to defendants. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO KAMEGAI KAPLA ET AL VS. ABBY XIAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for May 15, 2026, line 13. DEFENDANT ABBY JUN ZHANG'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Defendant Abby Zhang attempts to demur to the complaint's punitive damages allegations. There is no independent cause of action for punitive damages. Rather, punitive or exemplary damages are remedies available where a party pleads and proves the facts required under Civil Code, section 3294. "Punitive damages are merely incident to a cause of action and can never constitute the basis thereof." (Hilliard v. A.H. Robins Co. (1983) 148 Cal.App.3d 374, 391 [citation simplified].) The demurrer is overruled. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
